_comment: Machine-readable companion to rust-resolution.md. The rust_accuracy_gate report_matches_committed_doc test asserts these numbers equal the live differential (resolve_differential_graph over the committed Rust corpus), so the published report cannot silently drift. Regenerate the figures with: cargo test -p strata-index --test rust_accuracy_gate -- --nocapture print_rust_corpus_report. Regenerate the committed index.scip with: STRATA_SCIP_LIVE=1 cargo test -p strata-index --test gen_scip -- --ignored generate_rust
_by_band_comment: Per-confidence-band calibration (design §4.1): does an edge in band B resolve correctly at the rate B claims? precision is edge-level (confirmed/(confirmed+denied)) over SCIP-adjudicable edges; null = undefined (no adjudicable edges, never a vacuous 1.0). unadjudicable = sites in the band rust-analyzer cannot ground-truth (a .clone()/.len() on a std type, a Default::default-routed constructor), excluded from precision. RESOLVED carries no heuristic edge (the heuristic never claims rust-analyzer/compiler grade). Like Python, the Rust linker emits EXTRACTED-band edges for a same-file call. Resolving rust-analyzer impl-method monikers relies on the Track C1 shim in scip_merge::symbol_name_from_moniker.
